title: Per-tool renderers — Tier 1 (rich)
purpose: Tool calls are the primary activity of an agent session. Without per-tool rendering, all tool output is unstructured text. This brief defines the ToolRenderer trait and implements the Tier 1 (rich) renderers for tools that produce substantial, structured output: Bash, Edit (including rollback and override cases), ApplyPatch, Search, and Read.
task: Define the ToolRenderer trait in tools/mod.rs with header_line() and body() methods. Implement Tier 1 renderers in tools/rich.rs: Bash (streaming ANSI passthrough, exit code, duration), Edit (unified diff with red/green, blast-radius, AST BLOCKED rollback case, COMMITTED override case), ApplyPatch (diff hunks), Search (file:line:content with highlighted matches), Read (path + line range, body hidden by default). Each renderer produces a compact header line that doubles as the collapsed view.

requirements.title: ToolRenderer trait
requirements.spec: The ToolRenderer trait SHALL define the interface for all tool renderers with header_line() and body() for completed tool output (taking both call arguments and result), plus streaming_header() for in-progress display during ToolCallDelta accumulation.
acceptance: ToolRenderer trait with: header_line(&self, args: &Value, result: &Value, duration_ms: u64, caps: &TerminalCaps) -> String — args from ToolCallComplete or accumulated ToolCallDelta, result from ProviderEvent::ToolResult.output
ToolRenderer trait with: body(&self, args: &Value, result: &Value, caps: &TerminalCaps) -> Option<String>
ToolRenderer trait with: streaming_header(&self, name: &str, partial_args: &str, caps: &TerminalCaps) -> String — shown in the scroll region while the tool is executing, before ToolResult arrives
body() returns None for tools that have no expanded view
renderer_for(tool_name: &str) -> Option<Box<dyn ToolRenderer>> factory function
Test: renderer_for('bash') returns Some, renderer_for('unknown') returns None

requirements.title: Bash renderer
requirements.spec: The Bash renderer SHALL show the command with a $ prefix, stream output with ANSI passthrough, and display exit code and duration on completion.
acceptance: Header: '$ {command_preview}' truncated to terminal width, with exit code and duration on completion
Body: full command output with ANSI escape sequences passed through (subprocess colours preserved)
While running: header shows a spinner character
On completion: header shows exit code (0 or non-zero) and duration in seconds
Non-zero exit codes render with error styling
Test: header_line for a completed bash call includes exit code and duration

requirements.title: Edit renderer — success case
requirements.spec: WHEN an edit succeeds (committed: true, no AST errors), THE SYSTEM SHALL render a unified diff with red/green colouring and blast-radius symbols.
acceptance: Header: '~ {path}' with duration
Body: unified diff with removed lines in red (- prefix) and added lines in green (+ prefix)
Blast-radius section shows containing symbols from the tool output's blast_radius field
Test: header_line for a successful edit includes the file path

requirements.title: Edit renderer — AST rollback case
requirements.spec: WHEN an edit is rolled back due to AST validation failure (kind: 'edit_blocked_by_ast', committed: false), THE SYSTEM SHALL render the header with '✗' prefix and 'AST BLOCKED (not committed)' AND the body SHALL show diagnostic errors, NOT the diff.
acceptance: Header: '✗ ~ {path}  AST BLOCKED (not committed)'
Body: diagnostic error messages from the tool output's diagnostics array
The diff is NOT shown — showing a diff of uncommitted changes would be misleading
Test: header_line for a rolled-back edit contains 'AST BLOCKED'

requirements.title: Edit renderer — AST override case
requirements.spec: WHEN an edit is committed despite AST errors via AllowBrokenAst (committed: true, check_overrides non-empty), THE SYSTEM SHALL render the header with '⚠' prefix and show the override source attribution.
acceptance: Header: '⚠ ~ {path}  COMMITTED (AST override: {source})'
Source attribution comes from the first CheckOverride entry's source field
Body: unified diff (same as success case) plus diagnostic warnings below the diff
Test: header_line for an overridden edit contains the override source

requirements.title: ApplyPatch renderer
requirements.spec: The ApplyPatch renderer SHALL show diff hunks with +/- colouring.
acceptance: Header: 'patch {path}' with hunk count and duration
Body: diff hunks with + lines in green and - lines in red
Test: header_line includes the file path and hunk count

requirements.title: Search renderer
requirements.spec: The Search renderer SHALL show file:line:content results with highlighted matches grouped by file.
acceptance: Header: '? {query}' with result count and duration
Body: results grouped by file path, each result showing line number and content with the matched term highlighted
File path headers are styled distinctly from content lines
Test: header_line includes the query and result count

requirements.title: Read renderer
requirements.spec: The Read renderer SHALL show file path and line range AND SHALL hide the body by default.
acceptance: Header: '> {path}' with line range (e.g. 'lines 1-50') and duration
Body: returns Some (file content with line numbers) but the default verbosity level hides it
Body is shown only when the user has toggled verbose mode (Ctrl+O in NT-007)
Test: header_line includes the file path

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement Tier 2 (compact) or Tier 3 (minimal) renderers — that is NT-007
SHALL NOT implement the verbosity toggle (Ctrl+O) — that is NT-007
SHALL NOT implement the streaming event loop — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: ToolRenderer trait is object-safe — verified by Box<dyn ToolRenderer> compilation
renderer_for returns correct renderer for each Tier 1 tool — verified by test
Edit rollback shows AST BLOCKED, not diff — verified by test
Edit override shows source attribution — verified by test
Bash header includes exit code — verified by test
